title: Percentage Change
useCase: Use when a value increases or decreases compared with its original value.
example: If 80 becomes 100, change = 20, so percentage change = 20/80 × 100 = 25%.

title: Ratio Sharing
formula: One part = total / sum of ratio parts
useCase: Use when a total quantity is divided according to a ratio.
example: For 2:3 and total 50, one part = 50/5 = 10; shares are 20 and 30.

title: Average
formula: Average = sum of values / number of values
useCase: Use for mean score, combined values and data interpretation.

title: Speed
useCase: Use when distance and time are given and motion is uniform.
example: 60 meters in 5 seconds gives speed = 60/5 = 12 m/s.

title: Ohm's Law
useCase: Use for voltage, current and resistance problems in simple circuits.
example: If I = 2 A and R = 5 Ω, then V = 10 V.

title: Density
useCase: Use for basic physical property questions.
example: Mass 100 g and volume 20 cm³ gives density = 5 g/cm³.

title: Number of Moles
useCase: Use in basic stoichiometry and chemistry numericals.
example: 18 g water with molar mass 18 g/mol gives 1 mole.

title: Basic Probability
useCase: Use for simple chance and selection problems.
example: One head in a coin toss has probability 1/2.
